nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; typically found at depths of 1–15 m, closely associated with branching stony corals such as Stylophora pistillata, Acropora, and Porites

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a varied diet including meaty foods (mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, krill), spirulina-based herbivore preparations, and flake foods. Wild specimens tend to feed on microalgae, small crustaceans, and zooplankton.

summary: A hardy Red Sea damselfish with a striking white-and-black body edged in yellow and blue highlights. Generally considered beginner-friendly, though it tends to become territorial with age and is best kept with similarly assertive tankmates.
careNotes:
The Marginated Damselfish is endemic to the Red Sea and Gulf of Oman, where it is typically found sheltering among branching stony corals in shallow reef zones. It is generally considered one of the more manageable damsels due to its tolerance of a range of water conditions, but like most damselfishes it tends to become increasingly territorial as it matures. A group of juveniles can add color and activity to a reef aquarium, though aggression between individuals may increase over time in confined spaces.

Sources disagreed on adult size: LiveAquaria reported up to 4 inches (10.16 cm), while FishBase (scientific literature), ReefApp, and BlueZoo Aquatics each cited approximately 6 cm as the maximum total length. The averaged value of ~7 cm was used as the upper bound; FishBase's 6 cm TL maximum is the most scientifically grounded figure.

Sources also disagreed on minimum tank size: retail sources (LiveAquaria, BlueZoo) suggested 30 US gallons (~113 L), while ReefApp recommended 200 L. Given the species' territorial tendencies and the need for adequate rockwork territories, 150 L was chosen as a cautious round-number compromise erring toward the larger recommendation.

Temperature was averaged across LiveAquaria (72–78°F / 22.2–25.6°C), FishBase (24.5–29.1°C), and TankFacts (76–80°F / 24.4–26.7°C), yielding a recommended range of approximately 24–27°C.

FishBase notes this species is gonochoristic with non-functional hermaphroditism. ReefApp describes it as forming groups with one male and multiple females, and FishBase records monogamous mating behavior. It has been observed guarding and aerating benthic egg clutches, with males primarily responsible for egg care.

This species is reef-associated and generally considered reef safe; no sources documented coral nipping behavior.
breedingNotes: Has been observed spawning on hard substrates in the wild, with males guarding and aerating the demersal egg clutch. Captive breeding has been documented occasionally but is not commonly reported in the hobby literature. FishBase records both facultative and social monogamous mating behavior for this species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source. Reported adult size 4 inches, minimum tank 30 gal, temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level easy, temperament aggressive, reef safe yes.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing and water parameters. Reports max TL 6 cm, temperature 24.5–29.1°C, native range Western Indian Ocean/Red Sea/Gulf of Oman, family Pomacentridae, omnivorous trophic level 2.7, monogamous mating, benthic spawning with paternal egg care.

additional.name: ReefApp
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Reported adult size 6 cm, minimum tank 200 L, reef safe, SG ~1.026, notes aggressive tendencies and haremic/shoaling social structure.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Additional cross-check. Reported adult size 2.5 inches (~6.4 cm), minimum tank 30 gal, care level beginner, temperament semi-aggressive, reef safe yes, native range Red Sea.

fish.breedingNotes: Has been observed spawning on hard substrates in the wild, with males guarding and aerating the demersal egg clutch. Captive breeding has been documented occasionally but is not commonly reported in the hobby literature.
